ACL.summary: A container for Access List Control commands.

ACL CAT.summary: Lists the ACL categories, or the commands inside a category.
ACL CAT.complexity: O(1) since the categories and commands are a fixed set.

ACL DELUSER.summary: Deletes ACL users, and terminates their connections.
ACL DELUSER.complexity: O(1) amortized time considering the typical user.

ACL DRYRUN.summary: Simulates the execution of a command by a user, without executing the command.

ACL GENPASS.summary: Generates a pseudorandom, secure password that can be used to identify ACL users.

ACL GETUSER.summary: Lists the ACL rules of a user.
ACL GETUSER.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of password, command and pattern rules that the user has.
history: Added selectors and changed the format of key and channel patterns from a list to their rule representation.

ACL HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

ACL LIST.summary: Dumps the effective rules in ACL file format.
ACL LIST.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of configured users.

ACL LOAD.summary: Reloads the rules from the configured ACL file.
ACL LOAD.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of configured users.

ACL LOG.summary: Lists recent security events generated due to ACL rules.
ACL LOG.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of entries shown.
history: Added entry ID, timestamp created, and timestamp last updated.

ACL SAVE.summary: Saves the effective ACL rules in the configured ACL file.
ACL SAVE.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of configured users.

ACL SETUSER.summary: Creates and modifies an ACL user and its rules.
ACL SETUSER.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of rules provided.
history: Added selectors and key based permissions.

ACL USERS.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of configured users.

ACL WHOAMI.summary: Returns the authenticated username of the current connection.

APPEND.summary: Appends a string to the value of a key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
APPEND.complexity: O(1). The amortized time complexity is O(1) assuming the appended value is small and the already present value is of any size, since the dynamic string library used by Redis will double the free space available on every reallocation.

ARCOUNT.summary: Returns the number of non-empty elements in an array.

ARDEL.summary: Deletes elements at the specified indices in an array.
ARDEL.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of indices to delete

ARDELRANGE.summary: Deletes elements in one or more ranges.
ARDELRANGE.complexity: Proportional to the number of existing elements / slices touched, not to the numeric span of the requested ranges

ARGET.summary: Gets the value at an index in an array.

ARGETRANGE.summary: Gets values in a range of indices.
ARGETRANGE.complexity: O(N) where N is the range length

ARGREP.summary: Searches array elements in a range using textual predicates.
ARGREP.complexity: O(P * C) where P is the number of visited positions in touched slices and C is the cost of evaluating the predicates on one existing element.

ARINFO.complexity: O(1), or O(N) with FULL option where N is the number of slices.

ARINSERT.summary: Inserts one or more values at consecutive indices.
ARINSERT.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of values

ARLASTITEMS.summary: Returns the most recently inserted elements.
ARLASTITEMS.complexity: O(N) where N is the count

ARLEN.summary: Returns the length of an array (max index + 1).

ARMGET.summary: Gets values at multiple indices in an array.
ARMGET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of indices

ARMSET.summary: Sets multiple index-value pairs in an array.
ARMSET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of pairs

ARNEXT.summary: Returns the next index ARINSERT would use.

AROP.summary: Performs aggregate operations on array elements in a range.
AROP.complexity: O(P) where P is visited positions in touched slices (dense scanned slots + sparse entries), with worst-case O(|end-start|+1) and typical case close to O(N), where N is the number of existing elements in range.

ARRING.summary: Inserts values into a ring buffer of specified size, wrapping and truncating as needed.
ARRING.complexity: O(M) normally, O(N+M) on ring resize, where N is the maximum of the old and new ring size and M is the number of inserted values

ARSCAN.summary: Iterates existing elements in a range, returning index-value pairs.
ARSCAN.complexity: O(P) where P is visited positions in touched slices (dense scanned slots + sparse entries), with worst-case O(|end-start|+1) and typical case close to O(N), where N is the number of existing elements in range.

ARSEEK.summary: Sets the ARINSERT / ARRING cursor to a specific index.

ARSET.summary: Sets one or more contiguous values starting at an index in an array.
ARSET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of values

ASKING.summary: Signals that a cluster client is following an -ASK redirect.

AUTH.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of passwords defined for the user

BACKUP.summary: A container for backup management commands.

BACKUP ABORT.summary: Cancel a backup that has not been sealed yet.

BACKUP CLEANUP.summary: Remove a sealed backup's files and return to idle.

BACKUP HELP.summary: Return helpful text about BACKUP command parameters.

BACKUP LIST.summary: List the immutable backup file paths pinned so far.

BACKUP SEAL.summary: Freeze the current backup (BASE + INCR + manifest).

BACKUP START.summary: Start a new backup into the configured 'backupdirname'.

BF.ADD.summary: Adds an item to a Bloom Filter
BF.ADD.complexity: O(k), where k is the number of hash functions used by the last sub-filter

BF.CARD.summary: Returns the cardinality of a Bloom filter

BF.DEBUG.summary: Returns internal debug information about a Bloom Filter
BF.DEBUG.complexity: O(n), where n is the number of sub-filters

BF.EXISTS.summary: Checks whether an item exists in a Bloom Filter
BF.EXISTS.complexity: O(k), where k is the number of hash functions used by the last sub-filter

BF.INFO.summary: Returns information about a Bloom Filter

BF.INSERT.summary: Adds one or more items to a Bloom Filter. A filter will be created if it does not exist
BF.INSERT.complexity: O(k * n), where k is the number of hash functions and n is the number of items

BF.LOADCHUNK.summary: Restores a filter previously saved using SCANDUMP
BF.LOADCHUNK.complexity: O(n), where n is the capacity

BF.MADD.summary: Adds one or more items to a Bloom Filter. A filter will be created if it does not exist
BF.MADD.complexity: O(k * n), where k is the number of hash functions and n is the number of items

BF.MEXISTS.summary: Checks whether one or more items exist in a Bloom Filter
BF.MEXISTS.complexity: O(k * n), where k is the number of hash functions and n is the number of items

BF.SCANDUMP.summary: Begins an incremental save of the bloom filter
BF.SCANDUMP.complexity: O(n), where n is the capacity

BGREWRITEAOF.summary: Asynchronously rewrites the append-only file to disk.

BGSAVE.summary: Asynchronously saves the database(s) to disk.

BITCOUNT.summary: Counts the number of set bits (population counting) in a string.

BITFIELD.summary: Performs arbitrary bitfield integer operations on strings.

key_specs.notes: This command allows both access and modification of the key

BITFIELD_RO.summary: Performs arbitrary read-only bitfield integer operations on strings.

BITOP.summary: Performs bitwise operations on multiple strings, and stores the result.

BITPOS.summary: Finds the first set (1) or clear (0) bit in a string.

BLMOVE.summary: Pops an element from a list, pushes it to another list and returns it. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was moved.

BLMOVEM.summary: Moves up to (or exactly) a number of elements from one list to another and returns them. Blocks until the elements are available otherwise. Deletes the source list if it becomes empty.
BLMOVEM.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements moved.

BLMPOP.summary: Pops the first element from one of multiple lists. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
BLMPOP.complexity: O(N+M) where N is the number of provided keys and M is the number of elements returned.

BLPOP.summary: Removes and returns the first element in a list. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
BLPOP.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of provided keys.
history: `timeout` is interpreted as a double instead of an integer.

BRPOP.summary: Removes and returns the last element in a list. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
BRPOP.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of provided keys.
history: `timeout` is interpreted as a double instead of an integer.

BRPOPLPUSH.summary: Pops an element from a list, pushes it to another list and returns it. Block until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
history: `timeout` is interpreted as a double instead of an integer.

BZMPOP.summary: Removes and returns a member by score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member is available otherwise. Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.
BZMPOP.complexity: O(K) + O(M*log(N)) where K is the number of provided keys, N being the number of elements in the sorted set, and M being the number of elements popped.

BZPOPMAX.summary: Removes and returns the member with the highest score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member available otherwise.  Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.
BZPOPMAX.complexity: O(log(N)) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set.
history: `timeout` is interpreted as a double instead of an integer.

BZPOPMIN.summary: Removes and returns the member with the lowest score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member is available otherwise. Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.
BZPOPMIN.complexity: O(log(N)) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set.
history: `timeout` is interpreted as a double instead of an integer.

CF.ADD.summary: Adds an item to a Cuckoo Filter
CF.ADD.complexity: O(k + i), where k is the number of sub-filters and i is maxIterations

CF.ADDNX.summary: Adds an item to a Cuckoo Filter if the item did not exist previously.
CF.ADDNX.complexity: O(k + i), where k is the number of sub-filters and i is maxIterations

CF.COUNT.summary: Return the number of times an item might be in a Cuckoo Filter
CF.COUNT.complexity: O(k), where k is the number of sub-filters

CF.DEBUG.summary: Returns internal debug information about a Cuckoo Filter

CF.DEL.summary: Deletes an item from a Cuckoo Filter
CF.DEL.complexity: O(k), where k is the number of sub-filters

CF.EXISTS.summary: Checks whether one or more items exist in a Cuckoo Filter
CF.EXISTS.complexity: O(k), where k is the number of sub-filters

CF.INFO.summary: Returns information about a Cuckoo Filter

CF.INSERT.summary: Adds one or more items to a Cuckoo Filter. A filter will be created if it does not exist
CF.INSERT.complexity: O(n * (k + i)), where n is the number of items, k is the number of sub-filters and i is maxIterations

CF.INSERTNX.summary: Adds one or more items to a Cuckoo Filter if the items did not exist previously. A filter will be created if it does not exist
CF.INSERTNX.complexity: O(n * (k + i)), where n is the number of items, k is the number of sub-filters and i is maxIterations

CF.LOADCHUNK.summary: Restores a filter previously saved using SCANDUMP
CF.LOADCHUNK.complexity: O(n), where n is the capacity

CF.MEXISTS.summary: Checks whether one or more items exist in a Cuckoo Filter
CF.MEXISTS.complexity: O(k * n), where k is the number of sub-filters and n is the number of items

CF.SCANDUMP.summary: Begins an incremental save of the bloom filter
CF.SCANDUMP.complexity: O(n), where n is the capacity

CLIENT.summary: A container for client connection commands.

CLIENT CACHING.summary: Instructs the server whether to track the keys in the next request.

CLIENT GETNAME.summary: Returns the name of the connection.

CLIENT GETREDIR.summary: Returns the client ID to which the connection's tracking notifications are redirected.

CLIENT HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

CLIENT ID.summary: Returns the unique client ID of the connection.

CLIENT KILL.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of client connections
history: Replaced `slave` `TYPE` with `replica`. `slave` still supported for backward compatibility.

CLIENT LIST.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of client connections
history: Added `argv-mem`, `tot-mem`, `laddr` and `redir` fields and the optional `ID` filter.

CLIENT NO-EVICT.summary: Sets the client eviction mode of the connection.

CLIENT NO-TOUCH.summary: Controls whether commands sent by the client affect the LRU/LFU of accessed keys.

history: `CLIENT PAUSE WRITE` mode added along with the `mode` option.

CLIENT REPLY.summary: Instructs the server whether to reply to commands.

CLIENT SETINFO.summary: Sets information specific to the client or connection.

CLIENT TRACKING.summary: Controls server-assisted client-side caching for the connection.
CLIENT TRACKING.complexity: O(1). Some options may introduce additional complexity.

CLIENT TRACKINGINFO.summary: Returns information about server-assisted client-side caching for the connection.

CLIENT UNBLOCK.summary: Unblocks a client blocked by a blocking command from a different connection.
CLIENT UNBLOCK.complexity: O(log N) where N is the number of client connections

CLIENT UNPAUSE.summary: Resumes processing commands from paused clients.
CLIENT UNPAUSE.complexity: O(N) Where N is the number of paused clients

CLUSTER.summary: A container for Redis Cluster commands.

CLUSTER ADDSLOTS.summary: Assigns new hash slots to a node.
CLUSTER ADDSLOTS.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of hash slot arguments

CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE.summary: Assigns new hash slot ranges to a node.
CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of the slots between the start slot and end slot arguments.

CLUSTER COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS.summary: Returns the number of active failure reports active for a node.
CLUSTER COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of failure reports

CLUSTER COUNTKEYSINSLOT.summary: Returns the number of keys in a hash slot.

CLUSTER DELSLOTS.summary: Sets hash slots as unbound for a node.
CLUSTER DELSLOTS.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of hash slot arguments

CLUSTER DELSLOTSRANGE.summary: Sets hash slot ranges as unbound for a node.
CLUSTER DELSLOTSRANGE.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of the slots between the start slot and end slot arguments.

CLUSTER FAILOVER.summary: Forces a replica to perform a manual failover of its master.

CLUSTER FLUSHSLOTS.summary: Deletes all slots information from a node.

CLUSTER FORGET.summary: Removes a node from the nodes table.

CLUSTER GETKEYSINSLOT.summary: Returns the key names in a hash slot.
CLUSTER GETKEYSINSLOT.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of requested keys

CLUSTER HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

CLUSTER INFO.summary: Returns information about the state of a node.

CLUSTER KEYSLOT.summary: Returns the hash slot for a key.
CLUSTER KEYSLOT.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of bytes in the key

CLUSTER LINKS.summary: Returns a list of all TCP links to and from peer nodes.
CLUSTER LINKS.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of Cluster nodes

CLUSTER MEET.summary: Forces a node to handshake with another node.

CLUSTER MIGRATION.summary: Start, monitor and cancel slot migration.
CLUSTER MIGRATION.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of the slots between the start slot and end slot arguments.

CLUSTER MYID.summary: Returns the ID of a node.

CLUSTER MYSHARDID.summary: Returns the shard ID of a node.

CLUSTER NODES.summary: Returns the cluster configuration for a node.
CLUSTER NODES.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of Cluster nodes

CLUSTER REPLICAS.summary: Lists the replica nodes of a master node.
CLUSTER REPLICAS.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of replicas.

CLUSTER REPLICATE.summary: Configure a node as replica of a master node.

CLUSTER RESET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of known nodes. The command may execute a FLUSHALL as a side effect.

CLUSTER SAVECONFIG.summary: Forces a node to save the cluster configuration to disk.

CLUSTER SET-CONFIG-EPOCH.summary: Sets the configuration epoch for a new node.

CLUSTER SETSLOT.summary: Binds a hash slot to a node.

CLUSTER SHARDS.summary: Returns the mapping of cluster slots to shards.
CLUSTER SHARDS.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of cluster nodes

CLUSTER SLAVES.summary: Lists the replica nodes of a master node.
CLUSTER SLAVES.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of replicas.

CLUSTER SLOT-STATS.summary: Return an array of slot usage statistics for slots assigned to the current node.
CLUSTER SLOT-STATS.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of slots based on arguments. O(N*log(N)) with ORDERBY subcommand.

CLUSTER SLOTS.summary: Returns the mapping of cluster slots to nodes.
CLUSTER SLOTS.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of Cluster nodes

CLUSTER SYNCSLOTS.summary: Internal command for atomic slot migration protocol between cluster nodes.

CMS.INCRBY.summary: Increases the count of one or more items by increment
CMS.INCRBY.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of items

CMS.INITBYDIM.summary: Initializes a Count-Min Sketch to dimensions specified by user

CMS.INITBYPROB.summary: Initializes a Count-Min Sketch to accommodate requested tolerances.

CMS.MERGE.summary: Merges several sketches into one sketch
CMS.MERGE.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of sketches

CMS.QUERY.summary: Returns the count for one or more items in a sketch
CMS.QUERY.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of items

COMMAND.summary: Returns detailed information about all commands.
COMMAND.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of Redis commands

COMMAND DOCS.summary: Returns documentary information about one, multiple or all commands.
COMMAND DOCS.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of commands to look up

COMMAND GETKEYS.summary: Extracts the key names from an arbitrary command.
COMMAND GETKEYS.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of arguments to the command

COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS.summary: Extracts the key names and access flags for an arbitrary command.
COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of arguments to the command

COMMAND HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

COMMAND INFO.summary: Returns information about one, multiple or all commands.
COMMAND INFO.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of commands to look up
history: Allowed to be called with no argument to get info on all commands.

COMMAND LIST.summary: Returns a list of command names.
COMMAND LIST.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of Redis commands

CONFIG.summary: A container for server configuration commands.

CONFIG GET.summary: Returns the effective values of configuration parameters.
CONFIG GET.complexity: O(N) when N is the number of configuration parameters provided
history: Added the ability to pass multiple pattern parameters in one call

CONFIG HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

CONFIG REWRITE.summary: Persists the effective configuration to file.

CONFIG SET.complexity: O(N) when N is the number of configuration parameters provided
history: Added the ability to set multiple parameters in one call.

COPY.summary: Copies the value of a key to a new key.
COPY.complexity: O(N) worst case for collections, where N is the number of nested items. O(1) for string values.

DBSIZE.summary: Returns the number of keys in the database.

DECR.summary: Decrements the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

DECRBY.summary: Decrements a number from the integer value of a key. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

DEL.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys that will be removed. When a key to remove holds a value other than a string, the individual complexity for this key is O(M) where M is the number of elements in the list, set, sorted set or hash. Removing a single key that holds a string value is O(1).

DELEX.summary: Conditionally removes the specified key based on value or digest comparison.
DELEX.complexity: O(1) for IFEQ/IFNE, O(N) for IFDEQ/IFDNE where N is the length of the string value.

DIGEST.summary: Returns the XXH3 hash of a string value.
DIGEST.complexity: O(N) where N is the length of the string value.

DISCARD.complexity: O(N), when N is the number of queued commands

DUMP.summary: Returns a serialized representation of the value stored at a key.
DUMP.complexity: O(1) to access the key and additional O(N*M) to serialize it, where N is the number of Redis objects composing the value and M their average size. For small string values the time complexity is thus O(1)+O(1*M) where M is small, so simply O(1).

EVAL.complexity: Depends on the script that is executed.

key_specs.notes: We cannot tell how the keys will be used so we assume the worst, RW and UPDATE

EVALSHA.summary: Executes a server-side Lua script by SHA1 digest.
EVALSHA.complexity: Depends on the script that is executed.

EVALSHA_RO.summary: Executes a read-only server-side Lua script by SHA1 digest.
EVALSHA_RO.complexity: Depends on the script that is executed.

EVAL_RO.summary: Executes a read-only server-side Lua script.
EVAL_RO.complexity: Depends on the script that is executed.

key_specs.notes: We cannot tell how the keys will be used so we assume the worst, RO and ACCESS

EXEC.summary: Executes all commands in a transaction.
EXEC.complexity: Depends on commands in the transaction

EXISTS.summary: Determines whether one or more keys exist.
EXISTS.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to check.

EXPIRE.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key in seconds.

EXPIREAT.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key to a Unix timestamp.

EXPIRETIME.summary: Returns the expiration time of a key as a Unix timestamp.

FAILOVER.summary: Starts a coordinated failover from a server to one of its replicas.

FCALL.complexity: Depends on the function that is executed.

key_specs.notes: We cannot tell how the keys will be used so we assume the worst, RW and UPDATE

FCALL_RO.complexity: Depends on the function that is executed.

key_specs.notes: We cannot tell how the keys will be used so we assume the worst, RO and ACCESS

FLUSHALL.summary: Removes all keys from all databases.
FLUSHALL.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of keys in all databases

FLUSHDB.summary: Remove all keys from the current database.
FLUSHDB.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys in the selected database

FT.AGGREGATE.summary: Run a search query on an index and perform aggregate transformations on the results

arguments.name: index
arguments.summary: Specifies the name of the index. The index must be created using `FT.CREATE`.

arguments.name: query
arguments.summary: Specifies the query to profile and analyze performance.

arguments.name: verbatim
arguments.summary: Searches using the exact query terms without stemming or synonym expansion.

arguments.name: field
arguments.summary: Specifies a field in the index schema with its properties.

arguments.name: timeout
arguments.summary: Sets a time limit for query execution, specified in milliseconds.

arguments.name: groupby
arguments.summary: Groups results by specified fields, often used for aggregations.

arguments.name: exists
arguments.summary: Checks whether a field exists in a document.

arguments.name: log
arguments.summary: Return the logarithm of a number, property or subexpression

arguments.name: abs
arguments.summary: Return the absolute value of a numeric expression

arguments.name: ceil
arguments.summary: Round to the smallest integer not less than x

arguments.name: floor
arguments.summary: Round to largest integer not greater than x

arguments.name: log2
arguments.summary: Return the logarithm of x to base 2

arguments.name: exp
arguments.summary: Return the exponent of x, e.g., e^x

arguments.name: sqrt
arguments.summary: Return the square root of x

arguments.name: upper
arguments.summary: Return the uppercase conversion of s

arguments.name: lower
arguments.summary: Return the lowercase conversion of s

arguments.name: startswith
arguments.summary: Return 1 if s2 is the prefix of s1, 0 otherwise.

arguments.name: contains
arguments.summary: Return the number of occurrences of s2 in s1, 0 otherwise. If s2 is an empty string, return length(s1) + 1.

arguments.name: strlen
arguments.summary: Return the length of s

arguments.name: substr
arguments.summary: Return the substring of s, starting at offset and having count characters. If offset is negative, it represents the distance from the end of the string. If count is -1, it means "the rest of the string starting at offset".

arguments.name: format
arguments.summary: Use the arguments following fmt to format a string. Currently the only format argument supported is %s and it applies to all types of arguments.

arguments.name: matched_terms
arguments.summary: Return the query terms that matched for each record (up to 100), as a list. If a limit is specified, Redis will return the first N matches found, based on query order.

arguments.name: split
arguments.summary: Split a string by any character in the string sep, and strip any characters in strip. If only s is specified, it is split by commas and spaces are stripped. The output is an array.

arguments.name: timefmt
arguments.summary: Return a formatted time string based on a numeric timestamp value x.

arguments.name: parsetime
arguments.summary: The opposite of timefmt() - parse a time format using a given format string

arguments.name: day
arguments.summary: Round a Unix timestamp to midnight (00:00) start of the current day.

arguments.name: hour
arguments.summary: Round a Unix timestamp to the beginning of the current hour.

arguments.name: minute
arguments.summary: Round a Unix timestamp to the beginning of the current minute.

arguments.name: month
arguments.summary: Round a unix timestamp to the beginning of the current month.

arguments.name: dayofweek
arguments.summary: Convert a Unix timestamp to the day number (Sunday = 0).

arguments.name: dayofmonth
arguments.summary: Convert a Unix timestamp to the day of month number (1 .. 31).

arguments.name: dayofyear
arguments.summary: Convert a Unix timestamp to the day of year number (0 .. 365).

arguments.name: year
arguments.summary: Convert a Unix timestamp to the current year (e.g. 2018).

arguments.name: monthofyear
arguments.summary: Convert a Unix timestamp to the current month (0 .. 11).

arguments.name: geodistance
arguments.summary: Return distance in meters.

arguments.name: filter
arguments.summary: Applies a numeric range filter to restrict results to documents with field values within the specified range.

arguments.name: dialect
arguments.summary: Sets the query dialect version to be used.

FT.ALIASADD.summary: Adds an alias to the index

arguments.name: index
arguments.summary: Specifies the name of the index. The index must be created using `FT.CREATE`.

FT.ALIASDEL.summary: Deletes an alias from the index

FT.ALIASLIST.summary: Lists all aliases for the index
FT.ALIASLIST.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of aliases

arguments.name: index
arguments.summary: Specifies the name of the index. The index must be created using `FT.CREATE`.

FT.ALIASUPDATE.summary: Adds or updates an alias to the index

arguments.name: index
arguments.summary: Specifies the name of the index. The index must be created using `FT.CREATE`.

FT.ALTER.summary: Adds a new field to the index
FT.ALTER.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys in the keyspace

arguments.name: index
arguments.summary: Specifies the name of the index. The index must be created using `FT.CREATE`.

arguments.name: skipinitialscan
arguments.summary: Skips the initial scan of the database when creating the index.

arguments.name: schema
arguments.summary: Defines the fields in the index and their properties, such as type (`TEXT`, `TAG`, `NUMERIC`, etc.).

arguments.name: field
arguments.summary: Specifies a field in the index schema with its properties.

FT.CONFIG HELP.summary: Help description of runtime configuration options

FT.CREATE.summary: Creates an index with the given spec
FT.CREATE.complexity: O(K) at creation where K is the number of fields, O(N) if scanning the keyspace is triggered, where N is the number of keys in the keyspace
history: Added `PAYLOAD_FIELD` argument for backward support of `FT.SEARCH` deprecated `WITHPAYLOADS` argument

arguments.name: index
arguments.summary: Specifies the name of the index.

arguments.name: data_type
arguments.summary: Specifies the type of data to index, such as HASH or JSON.

arguments.name: indexall

GETRANGE.summary: Returns a substring of the string stored at a key.
GETRANGE.complexity: O(N) where N is the length of the returned string. The complexity is ultimately determined by the returned length, but because creating a substring from an existing string is very cheap, it can be considered O(1) for small strings.

GETSET.summary: Returns the previous string value of a key after setting it to a new value.

HDEL.summary: Deletes one or more fields and their values from a hash. Deletes the hash if no fields remain.
HDEL.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields to be removed.

HEXISTS.summary: Determines whether a field exists in a hash.

HEXPIRE.summary: Set expiry for hash field using relative time to expire (seconds)
HEXPIRE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

HEXPIREAT.summary: Set expiry for hash field using an absolute Unix timestamp (seconds)
HEXPIREAT.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

HEXPIRETIME.summary: Returns the expiration time of a hash field as a Unix timestamp, in seconds.
HEXPIRETIME.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

HGET.summary: Returns the value of a field in a hash.

HGETALL.summary: Returns all fields and values in a hash.
HGETALL.complexity: O(N) where N is the size of the hash.

HGETDEL.summary: Returns the value of a field and deletes it from the hash.
HGETDEL.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

HGETEX.summary: Get the value of one or more fields of a given hash key, and optionally set their expiration.
HGETEX.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

key_specs.notes: RW and UPDATE because it changes the TTL

HIMPORT.summary: A container for session-based hash import commands using fieldsets.

HIMPORT DISCARD.summary: Removes a single session-local fieldset by name.
HIMPORT DISCARD.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of session-local fieldsets.

HIMPORT DISCARDALL.summary: Removes all session-local fieldsets for the connection.
HIMPORT DISCARDALL.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of session-local fieldsets.

HIMPORT PREPARE.summary: Defines a session-local fieldset that maps a name to a sorted set of field names.
HIMPORT PREPARE.complexity: O(N*log(N)) where N is the number of fields.

HIMPORT SET.summary: Creates a fieldset-based hash from values supplied in the order matching a previously prepared fieldset.
HIMPORT SET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields in the fieldset.

HINCRBY.summary: Increments the integer value of a field in a hash by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the field doesn't exist.

HINCRBYFLOAT.summary: Increments the floating point value of a field by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the field doesn't exist.

HKEYS.summary: Returns all fields in a hash.
HKEYS.complexity: O(N) where N is the size of the hash.

HLEN.summary: Returns the number of fields in a hash.

HMGET.summary: Returns the values of all fields in a hash.
HMGET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields being requested.

HMSET.summary: Sets the values of multiple fields.
HMSET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields being set.

HOTKEYS.summary: A container for hotkeys tracking commands.

HOTKEYS GET.summary: Returns lists of top K hotkeys depending on metrics chosen in HOTKEYS START command.
HOTKEYS GET.complexity: O(K) where K is the number of hotkeys returned.

HOTKEYS HELP.summary: Return helpful text about HOTKEYS command parameters.

HOTKEYS RESET.summary: Release the resources used for hotkey tracking.

HPERSIST.summary: Removes the expiration time for each specified field
HPERSIST.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

HPEXPIRE.summary: Set expiry for hash field using relative time to expire (milliseconds)
HPEXPIRE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

HPEXPIREAT.summary: Set expiry for hash field using an absolute Unix timestamp (milliseconds)
HPEXPIREAT.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

HPEXPIRETIME.summary: Returns the expiration time of a hash field as a Unix timestamp, in msec.
HPEXPIRETIME.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

HPTTL.summary: Returns the TTL in milliseconds of a hash field.
HPTTL.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

HRANDFIELD.summary: Returns one or more random fields from a hash.
HRANDFIELD.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields returned

HSCAN.summary: Iterates over fields and values of a hash.
HSCAN.complexity: O(1) for every call. O(N) for a complete iteration, including enough command calls for the cursor to return back to 0. N is the number of elements inside the collection.

HSET.summary: Creates or modifies the value of a field in a hash.
HSET.complexity: O(1) for each field/value pair added, so O(N) to add N field/value pairs when the command is called with multiple field/value pairs.

HSETEX.summary: Set the value of one or more fields of a given hash key, and optionally set their expiration.
HSETEX.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields being set.

HSETNX.summary: Sets the value of a field in a hash only when the field doesn't exist.

HSTRLEN.summary: Returns the length of the value of a field.

HTTL.summary: Returns the TTL in seconds of a hash field.
HTTL.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields

HVALS.summary: Returns all values in a hash.
HVALS.complexity: O(N) where N is the size of the hash.

INCR.summary: Increments the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

INCRBY.summary: Increments the integer value of a key by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

INCRBYFLOAT.summary: Increment the floating point value of a key by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

INCREX.summary: Increments the numeric value of a key by a number and sets its expiration time. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

arguments.name: upperbound
