history: Added support for taking multiple section arguments.

JSON.ARRAPPEND.summary: Append the JSON values into the array at path after the last element in it
JSON.ARRAPPEND.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.ARRINDEX.summary: Search for the first occurrence of a JSON value in an array
JSON.ARRINDEX.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the array, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.ARRINSERT.summary: Insert the json values into the array at path before the index (shifts to the right)
JSON.ARRINSERT.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the array, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.ARRLEN.summary: Report the length of the JSON array at path in key
JSON.ARRLEN.complexity: O(1) where path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) where path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.ARRPOP.summary: Remove and return the element at the specified index in the array at path
JSON.ARRPOP.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the array and the specified index is not the last element, O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value and the specified index is the last element, or O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.ARRTRIM.summary: Trim an array so that it contains only the specified inclusive range of elements
JSON.ARRTRIM.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the array, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.CLEAR.summary: Clear container values (arrays/objects) and set numeric values to 0
JSON.CLEAR.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the values, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.DEBUG.summary: This is a container command for debugging related tasks

JSON.DEL.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the deleted value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.FORGET.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the deleted value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.GET.complexity: O(N) where N is the size of the JSON

JSON.MERGE.summary: Merge a given JSON value into matching paths. Consequently, JSON values at matching paths are updated, deleted, or expanded with new children
JSON.MERGE.complexity: O(M+N) when path is evaluated to a single value where M is the size of the original value (if it exists) and N is the size of the new value, O(M+N) when path is evaluated to multiple values where M is the size of the key and N is the size of the new value * the number of original values in the key

JSON.MGET.summary: Return the values at path from multiple key arguments
JSON.MGET.complexity: O(M*N) when path is evaluated to a single value where M is the number of keys and N is the size of the value, O(N1+N2+...+Nm) when path is evaluated to multiple values where m is the number of keys and Ni is the size of the i-th key

key_specs.notes: The key containing the JSON document

JSON.MSET.summary: Set or update one or more JSON values according to the specified key-path-value triplets
JSON.MSET.complexity: O(K*(M+N)) where k is the number of keys in the command, when path is evaluated to a single value where M is the size of the original value (if it exists) and N is the size of the new value, or O(K*(M+N)) when path is evaluated to multiple values where M is the size of the key and N is the size of the new value * the number of original values in the key

JSON.NUMINCRBY.summary: Increment the number value stored at path by number
JSON.NUMINCRBY.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.NUMMULTBY.summary: Multiply the number value stored at path by number
JSON.NUMMULTBY.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.NUMPOWBY.summary: Raise the number value stored at path to the power of number
JSON.NUMPOWBY.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.OBJKEYS.summary: Return the keys in the object that's referenced by path
JSON.OBJKEYS.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.OBJLEN.summary: Report the number of keys in the JSON object at path in key
JSON.OBJLEN.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.RESP.summary: Return the JSON in key in Redis serialization protocol specification form
JSON.RESP.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value, where N is the size of the value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.SET.summary: Set the JSON value at path in key
JSON.SET.complexity: O(M+N) where M is the size of the original value (if it exists) and N is the size of the new value

JSON.STRAPPEND.summary: Append the json-string values to the string at path
JSON.STRAPPEND.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.STRLEN.summary: Report the length of the JSON String at path in key
JSON.STRLEN.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.TOGGLE.summary: Toggle the boolean value stored at path
JSON.TOGGLE.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

JSON.TYPE.summary: Report the type of JSON value at path
JSON.TYPE.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key

KEYS.summary: Returns all key names that match a pattern.
KEYS.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of keys in the database, under the assumption that the key names in the database and the given pattern have limited length.

LASTSAVE.summary: Returns the Unix timestamp of the last successful save to disk.

LATENCY.summary: A container for latency diagnostics commands.

LATENCY DOCTOR.summary: Returns a human-readable latency analysis report.

LATENCY GRAPH.summary: Returns a latency graph for an event.

LATENCY HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

LATENCY HISTOGRAM.summary: Returns the cumulative distribution of latencies of a subset or all commands.
LATENCY HISTOGRAM.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of commands with latency information being retrieved.

LATENCY HISTORY.summary: Returns timestamp-latency samples for an event.

LATENCY LATEST.summary: Returns the latest latency samples for all events.

LATENCY RESET.summary: Resets the latency data for one or more events.

LCS.complexity: O(N*M) where N and M are the lengths of s1 and s2, respectively

LINDEX.summary: Returns an element from a list by its index.
LINDEX.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements to traverse to get to the element at index. This makes asking for the first or the last element of the list O(1).

LINSERT.summary: Inserts an element before or after another element in a list.
LINSERT.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements to traverse before seeing the value pivot. This means that inserting somewhere on the left end on the list (head) can be considered O(1) and inserting somewhere on the right end (tail) is O(N).

LLEN.summary: Returns the length of a list.

LMOVE.summary: Returns an element after popping it from one list and pushing it to another. Deletes the list if the last element was moved.

LMOVEM.summary: Moves up to (or exactly) a number of elements from one list to another and returns them. Deletes the source list if it becomes empty.
LMOVEM.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements moved.

LMPOP.summary: Returns multiple elements from a list after removing them. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
LMPOP.complexity: O(N+M) where N is the number of provided keys and M is the number of elements returned.

LOLWUT.summary: Displays computer art and the Redis version

LPOP.summary: Returns the first elements in a list after removing it. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
LPOP.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements returned

LPOS.summary: Returns the index of matching elements in a list.
LPOS.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements in the list, for the average case. When searching for elements near the head or the tail of the list, or when the MAXLEN option is provided, the command may run in constant time.

LPUSH.summary: Prepends one or more elements to a list. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
LPUSH.complexity: O(1) for each element added, so O(N) to add N elements when the command is called with multiple arguments.

LPUSHX.summary: Prepends one or more elements to a list only when the list exists.
LPUSHX.complexity: O(1) for each element added, so O(N) to add N elements when the command is called with multiple arguments.

LRANGE.summary: Returns a range of elements from a list.
LRANGE.complexity: O(S+N) where S is the distance of start offset from HEAD for small lists, from nearest end (HEAD or TAIL) for large lists; and N is the number of elements in the specified range.

LREM.summary: Removes elements from a list. Deletes the list if the last element was removed.
LREM.complexity: O(N+M) where N is the length of the list and M is the number of elements removed.

LSET.summary: Sets the value of an element in a list by its index.
LSET.complexity: O(N) where N is the length of the list. Setting either the first or the last element of the list is O(1).

LTRIM.summary: Removes elements from both ends a list. Deletes the list if all elements were trimmed.
LTRIM.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements to be removed by the operation.

MEMORY.summary: A container for memory diagnostics commands.

MEMORY HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

MEMORY MALLOC-STATS.complexity: Depends on how much memory is allocated, could be slow

MEMORY PURGE.summary: Asks the allocator to release memory.
MEMORY PURGE.complexity: Depends on how much memory is allocated, could be slow

MEMORY USAGE.summary: Estimates the memory usage of a key.
MEMORY USAGE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of samples.

MGET.summary: Atomically returns the string values of one or more keys.
MGET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to retrieve.

MIGRATE.summary: Atomically transfers a key from one Redis instance to another.
MIGRATE.complexity: This command actually executes a DUMP+DEL in the source instance, and a RESTORE in the target instance. See the pages of these commands for time complexity. Also an O(N) data transfer between the two instances is performed.

MODULE HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

MODULE LIST.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of loaded modules.

MODULE LOADEX.summary: Loads a module using extended parameters.

MONITOR.summary: Listens for all requests received by the server in real-time.

MOVE.summary: Moves a key to another database.

MSET.summary: Atomically creates or modifies the string values of one or more keys.
MSET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to set.

MSETEX.summary: Atomically sets multiple string keys with a shared expiration in a single operation. Supports flexible argument parsing where condition and expiration flags can appear in any order.
MSETEX.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to set.

MSETNX.summary: Atomically modifies the string values of one or more keys only when all keys don't exist.
MSETNX.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to set.

OBJECT.summary: A container for object introspection commands.

OBJECT ENCODING.summary: Returns the internal encoding of a Redis object.

OBJECT FREQ.summary: Returns the logarithmic access frequency counter of a Redis object.

OBJECT HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

OBJECT IDLETIME.summary: Returns the time since the last access to a Redis object.

OBJECT REFCOUNT.summary: Returns the reference count of a value of a key.

PERSIST.summary: Removes the expiration time of a key.

PEXPIRE.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key in milliseconds.

PEXPIREAT.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key to a Unix milliseconds timestamp.

PEXPIRETIME.summary: Returns the expiration time of a key as a Unix milliseconds timestamp.

PFADD.summary: Adds elements to a HyperLogLog key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

PFCOUNT.summary: Returns the approximated cardinality of the set(s) observed by the HyperLogLog key(s).
PFCOUNT.complexity: O(1) with a very small average constant time when called with a single key. O(N) with N being the number of keys, and much bigger constant times, when called with multiple keys.

key_specs.notes: RW because it may change the internal representation of the key, and propagate to replicas

PFDEBUG.summary: Internal commands for debugging HyperLogLog values.

PFMERGE.summary: Merges one or more HyperLogLog values into a single key.
PFMERGE.complexity: O(N) to merge N HyperLogLogs, but with high constant times.

PFSELFTEST.summary: An internal command for testing HyperLogLog values.

PSETEX.summary: Sets both string value and expiration time in milliseconds of a key. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

PSUBSCRIBE.summary: Listens for messages published to channels that match one or more patterns.
PSUBSCRIBE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of patterns to subscribe to.

PSYNC.summary: An internal command used in replication.

PTTL.summary: Returns the expiration time in milliseconds of a key.

PUBLISH.summary: Posts a message to a channel.
PUBLISH.complexity: O(N+M) where N is the number of clients subscribed to the receiving channel and M is the total number of subscribed patterns (by any client).

PUBSUB CHANNELS.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of active channels, and assuming constant time pattern matching (relatively short channels and patterns)

PUBSUB HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

PUBSUB NUMPAT.summary: Returns a count of unique pattern subscriptions.

PUBSUB NUMSUB.summary: Returns a count of subscribers to channels.
PUBSUB NUMSUB.complexity: O(N) for the NUMSUB subcommand, where N is the number of requested channels

PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of active shard channels, and assuming constant time pattern matching (relatively short shard channels).

PUBSUB SHARDNUMSUB.summary: Returns the count of subscribers of shard channels.
PUBSUB SHARDNUMSUB.complexity: O(N) for the SHARDNUMSUB subcommand, where N is the number of requested shard channels

PUNSUBSCRIBE.summary: Stops listening to messages published to channels that match one or more patterns.
PUNSUBSCRIBE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of patterns to unsubscribe.

RANDOMKEY.summary: Returns a random key name from the database.

READONLY.summary: Enables read-only queries for a connection to a Redis Cluster replica node.

READWRITE.summary: Enables read-write queries for a connection to a Reids Cluster replica node.

RENAME.summary: Renames a key and overwrites the destination.

RENAMENX.summary: Renames a key only when the target key name doesn't exist.
history: The command no longer returns an error when source and destination names are the same.

REPLCONF.summary: An internal command for configuring the replication stream.

REPLICAOF.summary: Configures a server as replica of another, or promotes it to a master.

RESTORE.summary: Creates a key from the serialized representation of a value.
RESTORE.complexity: O(1) to create the new key and additional O(N*M) to reconstruct the serialized value, where N is the number of Redis objects composing the value and M their average size. For small string values the time complexity is thus O(1)+O(1*M) where M is small, so simply O(1). However for sorted set values the complexity is O(N*M*log(N)) because inserting values into sorted sets is O(log(N)).

RESTORE-ASKING.summary: An internal command for migrating keys in a cluster.
RESTORE-ASKING.complexity: O(1) to create the new key and additional O(N*M) to reconstruct the serialized value, where N is the number of Redis objects composing the value and M their average size. For small string values the time complexity is thus O(1)+O(1*M) where M is small, so simply O(1). However for sorted set values the complexity is O(N*M*log(N)) because inserting values into sorted sets is O(log(N)).

RPOP.summary: Returns and removes the last elements of a list. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
RPOP.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements returned

RPOPLPUSH.summary: Returns the last element of a list after removing and pushing it to another list. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

RPUSH.summary: Appends one or more elements to a list. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
RPUSH.complexity: O(1) for each element added, so O(N) to add N elements when the command is called with multiple arguments.

RPUSHX.summary: Appends an element to a list only when the list exists.
RPUSHX.complexity: O(1) for each element added, so O(N) to add N elements when the command is called with multiple arguments.

SADD.summary: Adds one or more members to a set. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
SADD.complexity: O(1) for each element added, so O(N) to add N elements when the command is called with multiple arguments.

SAVE.summary: Synchronously saves the database(s) to disk.
SAVE.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of keys in all databases

SCAN.summary: Iterates over the key names in the database.
SCAN.complexity: O(1) for every call. O(N) for a complete iteration, including enough command calls for the cursor to return back to 0. N is the number of elements inside the collection.

SCARD.summary: Returns the number of members in a set.

SCRIPT.summary: A container for Lua scripts management commands.

SCRIPT DEBUG.summary: Sets the debug mode of server-side Lua scripts.

SCRIPT EXISTS.summary: Determines whether server-side Lua scripts exist in the script cache.
SCRIPT EXISTS.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of scripts to check (so checking a single script is an O(1) operation).

SCRIPT FLUSH.summary: Removes all server-side Lua scripts from the script cache.
SCRIPT FLUSH.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of scripts in cache
history: Added the `ASYNC` and `SYNC` flushing mode modifiers.

SCRIPT HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

SCRIPT KILL.summary: Terminates a server-side Lua script during execution.

SCRIPT LOAD.summary: Loads a server-side Lua script to the script cache.
SCRIPT LOAD.complexity: O(N) with N being the length in bytes of the script body.

SDIFF.summary: Returns the difference of multiple sets.
SDIFF.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.

SDIFFCARD.summary: Returns the number of members of the difference between the first set and all successive sets.
SDIFFCARD.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.

SDIFFSTORE.summary: Stores the difference of multiple sets in a key.
SDIFFSTORE.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.

SET.summary: Sets the string value of a key, ignoring its type. The key is created if it doesn't exist.
history: Allowed the `NX` and `GET` options to be used together.

key_specs.notes: RW and ACCESS due to the optional `GET` argument

SETBIT.summary: Sets or clears the bit at offset of the string value. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

SETEX.summary: Sets the string value and expiration time of a key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

SETNX.summary: Set the string value of a key only when the key doesn't exist.

SETRANGE.summary: Overwrites a part of a string value with another by an offset. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
SETRANGE.complexity: O(1), not counting the time taken to copy the new string in place. Usually, this string is very small so the amortized complexity is O(1). Otherwise, complexity is O(M) with M being the length of the value argument.

SHUTDOWN.summary: Synchronously saves the database(s) to disk and shuts down the Redis server.
SHUTDOWN.complexity: O(N) when saving, where N is the total number of keys in all databases when saving data, otherwise O(1)

SINTER.summary: Returns the intersect of multiple sets.
SINTER.complexity: O(N*M) worst case where N is the cardinality of the smallest set and M is the number of sets.

SINTERCARD.summary: Returns the number of members of the intersect of multiple sets.
SINTERCARD.complexity: O(N*M) worst case where N is the cardinality of the smallest set and M is the number of sets.

SINTERSTORE.summary: Stores the intersect of multiple sets in a key.
SINTERSTORE.complexity: O(N*M) worst case where N is the cardinality of the smallest set and M is the number of sets.

SISMEMBER.summary: Determines whether a member belongs to a set.

SLAVEOF.summary: Sets a Redis server as a replica of another, or promotes it to being a master.

SLOWLOG.summary: A container for slow log commands.

SLOWLOG GET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of entries returned
history: Added client IP address, port and name to the reply.
Added original command argument count to the reply.

SLOWLOG HELP.summary: Show helpful text about the different subcommands

SLOWLOG LEN.summary: Returns the number of entries in the slow log.

SLOWLOG RESET.summary: Clears all entries from the slow log.
SLOWLOG RESET.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of entries in the slowlog

SMEMBERS.summary: Returns all members of a set.
SMEMBERS.complexity: O(N) where N is the set cardinality.

SMISMEMBER.summary: Determines whether multiple members belong to a set.
SMISMEMBER.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements being checked for membership

SMOVE.summary: Moves a member from one set to another.

SORT.summary: Sorts the elements in a list, a set, or a sorted set, optionally storing the result.
SORT.complexity: O(N+M*log(M)) where N is the number of elements in the list or set to sort, and M the number of returned elements. When the elements are not sorted, complexity is O(N).

key_specs.notes: For the optional BY/GET keyword. It is marked 'unknown' because the key names derive from the content of the key we sort
For the optional STORE keyword. It is marked 'unknown' because the keyword can appear anywhere in the argument array

SORT_RO.summary: Returns the sorted elements of a list, a set, or a sorted set.
SORT_RO.complexity: O(N+M*log(M)) where N is the number of elements in the list or set to sort, and M the number of returned elements. When the elements are not sorted, complexity is O(N).

key_specs.notes: For the optional BY/GET keyword. It is marked 'unknown' because the key names derive from the content of the key we sort

SPOP.summary: Returns one or more random members from a set after removing them. Deletes the set if the last member was popped.
SPOP.complexity: Without the count argument O(1), otherwise O(N) where N is the value of the passed count.

SPUBLISH.summary: Post a message to a shard channel
SPUBLISH.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of clients subscribed to the receiving shard channel.

SRANDMEMBER.summary: Get one or multiple random members from a set
SRANDMEMBER.complexity: Without the count argument O(1), otherwise O(N) where N is the absolute value of the passed count.

SREM.summary: Removes one or more members from a set. Deletes the set if the last member was removed.
SREM.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of members to be removed.

SSCAN.summary: Iterates over members of a set.
SSCAN.complexity: O(1) for every call. O(N) for a complete iteration, including enough command calls for the cursor to return back to 0. N is the number of elements inside the collection.

SSUBSCRIBE.summary: Listens for messages published to shard channels.
SSUBSCRIBE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of shard channels to subscribe to.

STRLEN.summary: Returns the length of a string value.

SUBSCRIBE.summary: Listens for messages published to channels.
SUBSCRIBE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of channels to subscribe to.

SUBSTR.summary: Returns a substring from a string value.
SUBSTR.complexity: O(N) where N is the length of the returned string. The complexity is ultimately determined by the returned length, but because creating a substring from an existing string is very cheap, it can be considered O(1) for small strings.

SUNION.summary: Returns the union of multiple sets.
SUNION.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.

SUNIONCARD.summary: Returns the number of members of the union of multiple sets.
SUNIONCARD.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.

SUNIONSTORE.summary: Stores the union of multiple sets in a key.
SUNIONSTORE.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.

SUNSUBSCRIBE.summary: Stops listening to messages posted to shard channels.
SUNSUBSCRIBE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of shard channels to unsubscribe.

SWAPDB.complexity: O(N) where N is the count of clients watching or blocking on keys from both databases.

SYNC.summary: An internal command used in replication.

TDIGEST.ADD.summary: Adds one or more observations to a t-digest sketch
TDIGEST.ADD.complexity: O(N), where N is the number of samples to add

TDIGEST.BYRANK.summary: Returns, for each input rank, an estimation of the value (floating-point) with that rank
TDIGEST.BYRANK.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of ranks specified

TDIGEST.BYREVRANK.summary: Returns, for each input reverse rank, an estimation of the value (floating-point) with that reverse rank
TDIGEST.BYREVRANK.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of reverse ranks specified.

TDIGEST.CDF.summary: Returns, for each input value, an estimation of the floating-point fraction of (observations smaller than the given value + half the observations equal to the given value). Multiple fractions can be retrieved in a single call.
TDIGEST.CDF.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of values specified.

TDIGEST.CREATE.summary: Allocates memory and initializes a new t-digest sketch

TDIGEST.INFO.summary: Returns information and statistics about a t-digest sketch

TDIGEST.MAX.summary: Returns the maximum observation value from a t-digest sketch

TDIGEST.MERGE.summary: Merges multiple t-digest sketches into a single sketch
TDIGEST.MERGE.complexity: O(N*K), where N is the number of centroids and K being the number of input sketches

TDIGEST.MIN.summary: Returns the minimum observation value from a t-digest sketch

TDIGEST.QUANTILE.summary: Returns, for each input fraction, an estimation of the value (floating point) that is smaller than the given fraction of observations
TDIGEST.QUANTILE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of quantiles specified.

TDIGEST.RANK.summary: Returns, for each input value (floating-point), the estimated rank of the value (the number of observations in the sketch that are smaller than the value + half the number of observations that are equal to the value)
TDIGEST.RANK.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of values specified.

TDIGEST.RESET.summary: Resets a t-digest sketch: empty the sketch and re-initializes it.

TDIGEST.REVRANK.summary: Returns, for each input value (floating-point), the estimated reverse rank of the value (the number of observations in the sketch that are larger than the value + half the number of observations that are equal to the value)
TDIGEST.REVRANK.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of values specified.

TDIGEST.TRIMMED_MEAN.summary: Returns an estimation of the mean value from the sketch, excluding observation values outside the low and high cutoff quantiles
TDIGEST.TRIMMED_MEAN.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of centroids

TOPK.ADD.summary: Adds an item to a Top-k sketch. Multiple items can be added at the same time.
TOPK.ADD.complexity: O(n * k) where n is the number of items and k is the depth

TOPK.COUNT.summary: Return the count for one or more items are in a sketch
TOPK.COUNT.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of items
history: As of Bloom version 2.4, this command is regarded as deprecated. The count value is not a representative of the number of appearances of an item

TOPK.INCRBY.summary: Increases the count of one or more items by increment
TOPK.INCRBY.complexity: O(n * k * incr) where n is the number of items, k is the depth and incr is the increment

TOPK.LIST.summary: Return the full list of items in Top-K sketch.
TOPK.LIST.complexity: O(k*log(k)) where k is the value of top-k

TOPK.QUERY.summary: Checks whether one or more items are in a sketch
TOPK.QUERY.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of items

TOPK.RESERVE.summary: Initializes a Top-K sketch with specified parameters

TOUCH.summary: Returns the number of existing keys out of those specified after updating the time they were last accessed.
TOUCH.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys that will be touched.

TRIMSLOTS.summary: Trim the keys that belong to specified slots.
TRIMSLOTS.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of keys in all databases

TS.ADD.summary: Append a sample to a time series
TS.ADD.complexity: O(M) where M is the number of compaction rules or O(1) with no compaction

TS.ALTER.summary: Update the retention, chunk size, duplicate policy, and labels of an existing time series
TS.ALTER.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of labels requested to update

key_specs.notes: Source time series key
Destination time series key

TS.DECRBY.summary: Decrease the value of the latest sample
TS.DECRBY.complexity: O(M) when M is the amount of compaction rules or O(1) with no compaction

TS.DEL.summary: Delete all samples between two timestamps for a given time series
TS.DEL.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of data points that will be removed

TS.GET.summary: Get the sample with the highest timestamp from a given time series

TS.INCRBY.summary: Increase the value of the latest sample
TS.INCRBY.complexity: O(M) when M is the amount of compaction rules or O(1) with no compaction

TS.INFO.summary: Returns information and statistics for a time series

TS.MADD.summary: Append new samples to one or more time series
TS.MADD.complexity: O(N*M) when N is the amount of series updated and M is the amount of compaction rules or O(N) with no compaction

TS.MGET.summary: Get the sample with the highest timestamp from each time series matching a specific filter
TS.MGET.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of time-series that match the filters

TS.MRANGE.summary: Query a range across multiple time series by filters in forward direction
TS.MRANGE.complexity: O(n/m+k) where n = Number of data points, m = Chunk size (data points per chunk), k = Number of data points that are in the requested ranges

TS.MREVRANGE.summary: Query a range across multiple time series by filters in reverse direction
TS.MREVRANGE.complexity: O(n/m+k) where n = Number of data points, m = Chunk size (data points per chunk), k = Number of data points that are in the requested ranges

TS.NRANGE.summary: Query a range across multiple time series in forward direction, returning the results pivoted by timestamp (one value column per key)
TS.NRANGE.complexity: O(numkeys*(n/m+k)) where n = Number of samples, m = Chunk size (samples per chunk), k = Number of samples that are in the requested range

TS.NREVRANGE.summary: Query a range across multiple time series in reverse direction, returning the results pivoted by timestamp (one value column per key)
TS.NREVRANGE.complexity: O(numkeys*(n/m+k)) where n = Number of samples, m = Chunk size (samples per chunk), k = Number of samples that are in the requested range

TS.QUERYINDEX.summary: Get all time series keys matching a filter list
TS.QUERYINDEX.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of time-series that match the filters

TS.QUERYLABELS.summary: Get all label names, or all values of a given label, for time series matching a filter list, or all series
TS.QUERYLABELS.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of time-series that match the filters (all indexed series when FILTER is omitted)

TS.RANGE.summary: Query a range in forward direction
TS.RANGE.complexity: O(n/m+k) where n = Number of data points, m = Chunk size (data points per chunk), k = Number of data points that are in the requested range

TS.READ.summary: Read: return up to max_count samples with timestamp >= timestamp. With BLOCK, waits up to milliseconds ms until at least min_count qualifying samples exist
TS.READ.complexity: O(log(n)+k) where n is the number of samples in the series and k is the number of returned samples

TS.REVRANGE.summary: Query a range in reverse direction
TS.REVRANGE.complexity: O(n/m+k) where n = Number of data points, m = Chunk size (data points per chunk), k = Number of data points that are in the requested range

TTL.summary: Returns the expiration time in seconds of a key.

TYPE.summary: Determines the type of value stored at a key.

UNLINK.summary: Asynchronously deletes one or more keys.
UNLINK.complexity: O(1) for each key removed regardless of its size. Then the command does O(N) work in a different thread in order to reclaim memory, where N is the number of allocations the deleted objects where composed of.

UNSUBSCRIBE.summary: Stops listening to messages posted to channels.
UNSUBSCRIBE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of channels to unsubscribe.

UNWATCH.summary: Forgets about watched keys of a transaction.

VADD.summary: Add one or more elements to a vector set, or update its vector if it already exists

VCARD.summary: Return the number of elements in a vector set

VDIM.summary: Return the dimension of vectors in the vector set

VEMB.summary: Return the vector associated with an element

VGETATTR.summary: Retrieve the JSON attributes of elements

VINFO.summary: Return information about a vector set

VISMEMBER.summary: Check if an element exists in a vector set

VLINKS.summary: Return the neighbors of an element at each layer in the HNSW graph

VRANDMEMBER.summary: Return one or multiple random members from a vector set

VRANGE.summary: Return vector set elements in a lex range

VREM.summary: Remove an element from a vector set

VSETATTR.summary: Associate or remove the JSON attributes of elements

WAIT.summary: Blocks until the asynchronous replication of all preceding write commands sent by the connection is completed.

WAITAOF.summary: Blocks until all of the preceding write commands sent by the connection are written to the append-only file of the master and/or replicas.

WATCH.summary: Monitors changes to keys to determine the execution of a transaction.

XACK.summary: Returns the number of messages that were successfully acknowledged by the consumer group member of a stream.
XACK.complexity: O(1) for each message ID processed.

XACKDEL.summary: Acknowledges and deletes one or multiple messages for a stream consumer group.
XACKDEL.complexity: O(1) for each message ID processed.

XADD.summary: Appends a new message to a stream. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
XADD.complexity: O(1) when adding a new entry, O(N) when trimming where N being the number of entries evicted.
history: Added the `NOMKSTREAM` option, `MINID` trimming strategy and the `LIMIT` option.
Added support for the `<ms>-*` explicit ID form.

key_specs.notes: UPDATE instead of INSERT because of the optional trimming feature

XAUTOCLAIM.summary: Changes, or acquires, ownership of messages in a consumer group, as if the messages were delivered to as consumer group member.
history: Added an element to the reply array, containing deleted entries the command cleared from the PEL

XCFGSET.summary: Sets the IDMP configuration parameters for a stream.

XCLAIM.summary: Changes, or acquires, ownership of a message in a consumer group, as if the message was delivered a consumer group member.
XCLAIM.complexity: O(log N) with N being the number of messages in the PEL of the consumer group.

XDEL.summary: Returns the number of messages after removing them from a stream.
XDEL.complexity: O(1) for each single item to delete in the stream, regardless of the stream size.

XDELEX.summary: Deletes one or multiple entries from the stream.
XDELEX.complexity: O(1) for each single item to delete in the stream, regardless of the stream size.

XGROUP.summary: A container for consumer groups commands.

XGROUP CREATECONSUMER.summary: Creates a consumer in a consumer group.

XGROUP DELCONSUMER.summary: Deletes a consumer from a consumer group.

XGROUP DESTROY.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of entries in the group's pending entries list (PEL).

XGROUP HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

XGROUP SETID.summary: Sets the last-delivered ID of a consumer group.

XIDMPRECORD.summary: An internal command for setting IDMP metadata on an existing stream message.

XINFO.summary: A container for stream introspection commands.

XINFO CONSUMERS.summary: Returns a list of the consumers in a consumer group.
history: Added the `inactive` field, and changed the meaning of `idle`.

XINFO GROUPS.summary: Returns a list of the consumer groups of a stream.

XINFO HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

history: Added the `active-time` field, and changed the meaning of `seen-time`.
Added the `idmp-duration`, `idmp-maxsize`, `pids-tracked`, `iids-tracked`, `iids-added` and `iids-duplicates` fields for IDMP tracking.
Added the `nacked-count` field to consumer groups in `FULL` output.

XLEN.summary: Return the number of messages in a stream.

XNACK.summary: Releases claimed messages back to the group's PEL without acknowledging them, making them available for re-delivery.
XNACK.complexity: O(1) for each message ID processed.

XPENDING.summary: Returns the information and entries from a stream consumer group's pending entries list.
XPENDING.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of elements returned, so asking for a small fixed number of entries per call is O(1). O(M), where M is the total number of entries scanned when used with the IDLE filter. When the command returns just the summary and the list of consumers is small, it runs in O(1) time; otherwise, an additional O(N) time for iterating every consumer.
history: Added the `IDLE` option and exclusive range intervals.

XRANGE.summary: Returns the messages from a stream within a range of IDs.
XRANGE.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of elements being returned. If N is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with COUNT), you can consider it O(1).

XREAD.summary: Returns messages from multiple streams with IDs greater than the ones requested. Blocks until a message is available otherwise.

key_specs.notes: Incomplete because a stream key named STREAMS (or options before it) can shift the STREAMS keyword; fall back to xreadGetKeys

XREADGROUP.summary: Returns new or historical messages from a stream for a consumer in a group. Blocks until a message is available otherwise.
XREADGROUP.complexity: For each stream mentioned: O(M) with M being the number of elements returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with COUNT), you can consider it O(1). On the other side when XREADGROUP blocks, XADD will pay the O(N) time in order to serve the N clients blocked on the stream getting new data.

key_specs.notes: Incomplete because a consumer/group named STREAMS (or options before GROUP) can shift the STREAMS keyword; fall back to xreadGetKeys

XREVRANGE.summary: Returns the messages from a stream within a range of IDs in reverse order.
XREVRANGE.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of elements returned. If N is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with COUNT), you can consider it O(1).

XSETID.summary: An internal command for replicating stream values.

XTRIM.summary: Deletes messages from the beginning of a stream.
XTRIM.complexity: O(N), with N being the number of evicted entries. Constant times are very small however, since entries are organized in macro nodes containing multiple entries that can be released with a single deallocation.
history: Added the `MINID` trimming strategy and the `LIMIT` option.

ZADD.summary: Adds one or more members to a sorted set, or updates their scores. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
ZADD.complexity: O(log(N)) for each item added, where N is the number of elements in the sorted set.

ZCARD.summary: Returns the number of members in a sorted set.

ZCOUNT.summary: Returns the count of members in a sorted set that have scores within a range.
ZCOUNT.complexity: O(log(N)) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set.

ZDIFF.summary: Returns the difference between multiple sorted sets.
ZDIFF.complexity: O(L + (N-K)log(N)) worst case where L is the total number of elements in all the sets, N is the size of the first set, and K is the size of the result set.

ZDIFFSTORE.summary: Stores the difference of multiple sorted sets in a key.
ZDIFFSTORE.complexity: O(L + (N-K)log(N)) worst case where L is the total number of elements in all the sets, N is the size of the first set, and K is the size of the result set.

ZINCRBY.summary: Increments the score of a member in a sorted set.
ZINCRBY.complexity: O(log(N)) where N is the number of elements in the sorted set.

ZINTER.summary: Returns the intersect of multiple sorted sets.
ZINTER.complexity: O(N*K)+O(M*log(M)) worst case with N being the smallest input sorted set, K being the number of input sorted sets and M being the number of elements in the resulting sorted set.

ZINTERCARD.summary: Returns the number of members of the intersect of multiple sorted sets.
ZINTERCARD.complexity: O(N*K) worst case with N being the smallest input sorted set, K being the number of input sorted sets.

ZINTERSTORE.summary: Stores the intersect of multiple sorted sets in a key.
ZINTERSTORE.complexity: O(N*K)+O(M*log(M)) worst case with N being the smallest input sorted set, K being the number of input sorted sets and M being the number of elements in the resulting sorted set.

ZLEXCOUNT.summary: Returns the number of members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range.
ZLEXCOUNT.complexity: O(log(N)) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set.

ZMPOP.summary: Returns the highest- or lowest-scoring members from one or more sorted sets after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.
ZMPOP.complexity: O(K) + O(M*log(N)) where K is the number of provided keys, N being the number of elements in the sorted set, and M being the number of elements popped.

ZMSCORE.summary: Returns the score of one or more members in a sorted set.
ZMSCORE.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of members being requested.

ZPOPMAX.summary: Returns the highest-scoring members from a sorted set after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.
ZPOPMAX.complexity: O(log(N)*M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set, and M being the number of elements popped.

ZPOPMIN.summary: Returns the lowest-scoring members from a sorted set after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.
ZPOPMIN.complexity: O(log(N)*M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set, and M being the number of elements popped.

ZRANDMEMBER.summary: Returns one or more random members from a sorted set.
ZRANDMEMBER.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of members returned

ZRANGE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes.
ZRANGE.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements returned.

ZRANGEBYLEX.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range.
ZRANGEBYLEX.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements being returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with LIMIT), you can consider it O(log(N)).

ZRANGEBYSCORE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores.
ZRANGEBYSCORE.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements being returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with LIMIT), you can consider it O(log(N)).

ZRANGESTORE.summary: Stores a range of members from sorted set in a key.
ZRANGESTORE.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements stored into the destination key.

ZRANK.summary: Returns the index of a member in a sorted set ordered by ascending scores.

ZREM.summary: Removes one or more members from a sorted set. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.
ZREM.complexity: O(M*log(N)) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements to be removed.

ZREMRANGEBYLEX.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.
ZREMRANGEBYLEX.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements removed by the operation.

ZREMRANGEBYRANK.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of indexes. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.
ZREMRANGEBYRANK.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements removed by the operation.

ZREMRANGEBYSCORE.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of scores. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.
ZREMRANGEBYSCORE.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements removed by the operation.

ZREVRANGE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes in reverse order.
ZREVRANGE.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements returned.

ZREVRANGEBYLEX.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range in reverse order.
ZREVRANGEBYLEX.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements being returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with LIMIT), you can consider it O(log(N)).

ZREVRANGEBYSCORE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores in reverse order.
ZREVRANGEBYSCORE.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements being returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with LIMIT), you can consider it O(log(N)).

ZREVRANK.summary: Returns the index of a member in a sorted set ordered by descending scores.

ZSCAN.summary: Iterates over members and scores of a sorted set.
ZSCAN.complexity: O(1) for every call. O(N) for a complete iteration, including enough command calls for the cursor to return back to 0. N is the number of elements inside the collection.

ZSCORE.summary: Returns the score of a member in a sorted set.

ZUNION.summary: Returns the union of multiple sorted sets.
ZUNION.complexity: O(N)+O(M*log(M)) with N being the sum of the sizes of the input sorted sets, and M being the number of elements in the resulting sorted set.

ZUNIONSTORE.summary: Stores the union of multiple sorted sets in a key.
ZUNIONSTORE.complexity: O(N)+O(M log(M)) with N being the sum of the sizes of the input sorted sets, and M being the number of elements in the resulting sorted set.

_FT.CONFIG HELP.summary: Help description of runtime configuration options
